# Apple News Format Release Notes

**Article**

Learn about new features that require iOS 26, iPadOS 26, and macOS 26.

## Overview

This feature was added for the latest Apple News Format release:

- In the [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/Metadata] object, use `syndicated` to indicate a syndication or wire service produced the content.

These features were added in a previous Apple News Format release:

- You can embed a Facebook reel in your article using the `URL` property in the [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/FacebookPost] object.
- In the [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/Condition] object, use `following` to indicate whether the person viewing the article is following the channel that published the article.

### Deprecated Objects and Properties

This property was deprecated in the latest Apple News Format release:

- The `transparentToolbar` property in the [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/Metadata] object was deprecated.

These objects were deprecated in a previous Apple News Format release:

- [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/AutoPlacement], [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/AdvertisementAutoPlacement], [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/ConditionalAutoPlacement], and [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/AutoPlacementLayout] objects are deprecated because you can only control advertisement settings via News Publisher.
- [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/MediumRectangleAdvertisement] and [doc://com.apple.applenews/documentation/AppleNewsFormat/BannerAdvertisement] objects are deprecated because Apple News no longer supports manual ads.

## Release Notes

- **Apple News Format Version History**: Learn how Apple News Format versions map to iOS, iPadOS, macOS, and visionOS releases.
